summary: Mass rename of legacy item split function symbols from translation-unit/address-style prefixes such as `it_3F14_Logic11_Dropped` and `it_2725_Logic67_EvtUnk` to item-specific names derived from the current item filenames, such as `itBat_Logic11_Dropped`, `itCapsule_Logic0_Spawned`, and `itMewtwoDisable_Logic67_EvtUnk`. The rename was propagated through item source files, item headers, and `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`. The decomp.dev report showed no GALE01 changes, indicating this was intended as a symbol/name cleanup rather than a behavior or matching change.
what_changed: Renamed many old split-item functions whose prefixes still reflected split files or old translation units, especially `it_3F14_*` and `it_2725_*` names.

Converted function names to item-specific prefixes based on current item filenames, e.g. `it_3F14_Logic11_Dropped` became `itBat_Logic11_Dropped` and `it_3F14_Logic0_Spawned` became `itCapsule_Logic0_Spawned`.
Updated function definitions and prototypes in item `.c` and `.h` files so declarations matched the new names.
Updated `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` after review feedback so global symbol names matched the source-level renames.
Touched a very broad item surface area: 287 changed files with near-equal additions and deletions, consistent with mechanical renaming.

Preserved the existing item callback suffix vocabulary such as `Spawned`, `Destroyed`, `PickedUp`, `Dropped`, `Thrown`, `EnteredAir`, `DmgDealt`, `DmgReceived`, `Clanked`, `Reflected`, `HitShield`, `Absorbed`, `ShieldBounced`, and `EvtUnk`.
smart_moves: Used current filenames as the source of truth for item prefixes, making names searchable by item module instead of by obsolete split-unit identifiers.
Kept the existing `LogicNN_Event` structure intact while only replacing the stale prefix, minimizing semantic risk.
Propagated the rename through symbols, source, and headers, which is necessary for decomp projects where `symbols.txt` and C identifiers must stay aligned.
Responded to review feedback by adding the missing `symbols.txt` update before merge.
The bot report showed `No changes` for GALE01, supporting that the rename did not perturb the built output.
decomp_lessons: For large mechanical symbol renames, update `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` at the same time as `.c` definitions and `.h` declarations; otherwise reviewers may block the PR even if source compiles.

When an item module has already been split into per-item files, stale prefixes like `it_3F14_` or `it_2725_` should be replaced with the item module prefix to improve navigation and future matching work.
Name-only decomp cleanups should preserve callback suffixes and logic IDs unless there is evidence for a stronger semantic name.
A no-op decomp.dev report is useful evidence that broad symbol churn remained behavior-neutral.
Use tooling for bulk renames rather than hand-editing hundreds of references; reviewer specifically pointed to `melee-replace-symbols` and `rename_tu`.
naming_conventions: Examples from the diff include `itCapsule_Logic0_Spawned`, `itBat_Logic11_Dropped`, `itSword_Logic12_Thrown`, `itHeart_Logic8_PickedUp`, `itTomato_Logic9_EvtUnk`, and `itMewtwoDisable_Logic67_EvtUnk`.

The PR retained established callback event suffixes: `Spawned`, `Destroyed`, `PickedUp`, `Dropped`, `Thrown`, `EnteredAir`, `DmgDealt`, `DmgReceived`, `Clanked`, `Reflected`, `HitShield`, `Absorbed`, `ShieldBounced`, `EvtUnk`.
The evidence supports item filename-derived prefixes, but does not establish that every renamed symbol was semantically verified beyond file/module association.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This was a mechanical rename rather than an assembly-matching rewrite; the added and deleted line counts were nearly identical.
Reviewer recommended `melee-replace-symbols` for applying `from:to` rename pairs consistently across the repository.
Reviewer also mentioned the `rename_tu` function in `tools/functions.sh` as a reusable helper for translation-unit renames.
The decomp.dev GALE01 report said `No changes`, suggesting the tactic preserved binary output.

review_feedback.feedback: Update `symbols.txt`; recommended `melee-replace-symbols` and a generated list of `from:to` pairs to ensure symbols are renamed everywhere.
Mentioned that `tools/functions.sh` contains a sourceable `rename_tu` function.

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.feedback: Acknowledged the missing committed file: `whoops, I actually had, I just forgot to commit that file`.
Approved after the missing symbol update was addressed.

searchable_terms: Logic Spawned PickedUp Dropped Thrown DmgDealt EvtUnk
follow_up_queries: Search for item callbacks still using translation-unit prefixes instead of item prefixes
Look up PR #772 for `melee-replace-symbols` usage guidance
Inspect `tools/functions.sh` `rename_tu` for future bulk TU renames
